findings.evidence: Lines 45-55 expose the bodies; `rg -n "ConditionallyIIDWith\.intro|ConditionallyIID\.intro|ConditionallyIIDWith_def|ConditionallyIID_def" code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability` finds no introduction or def-restatement lemmas.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from these public predicates and add stable characteristic API such as `ConditionallyIIDWith.intro`, `ConditionallyIID.intro`/`of_directing`, and `*_def`/iff restatements.
findings.issue: `ConditionallyIIDWith` and `ConditionallyIID` are both marked `@[expose]`, but the API only supplies eliminators; users must rely on the exposed conjunction/existential bodies to construct or restate the predicates.

findings.evidence: Lines 58-68 define the two eliminators with no attributes; these are exactly the public facts unpacking the predicate.
findings.fix: Add the appropriate `@[grind]` annotations to the characteristic eliminators, especially `ConditionallyIIDWith.measurable_directing` and `ConditionallyIIDWith.finite_map_eq`.
findings.issue: The characteristic projection lemmas for `ConditionallyIIDWith` are unannotated, so automation will not use a conditional-i.i.d. hypothesis to recover measurability or the finite-block mixture identity.

summary: The new directing-measure predicates expose their bodies while providing only destructors, so downstream construction/rewriting is tied to unfolding rather than a stable API. The characteristic eliminators also lack the automation annotations expected for this public predicate API.
